steps.title: Read the marked angle and connect it to triangle XYZ
steps.content: The diagram marks ∠A = 60° inside triangle ABC. Triangle XYZ is a dilation of triangle ABC with A $\Rightarrow$ X, B $\Rightarrow$ Y, C $\Rightarrow$ Z, so the triangles are similar.

steps.title: Apply the similar-triangles angle property
steps.content: Dilation preserves all angle measures, so corresponding angles are equal: ∠X = ∠A = 60°. That matches choice C - C is correct.
